name: IBudgetEnforcer
description: Contract for per-workflow budget enforcement implementations. Tracks cumulative token/cost usage across workflow steps and reports the current budget state. The executor checks the budget after each step and triggers degradation or hard-stop behaviour accordingly.

methods.name: track_usage

params.name: usage
params.description: Usage dict from the LLM provider response, typically containing prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, and total_cost keys

methods.description: Record token/cost usage for a completed step.

methods.name: check_budget
methods.description: Return the current budget state. Returns a BudgetStatus indicating whether the workflow is within budget, degraded, or exceeded.

methods.name: get_remaining
methods.description: Return the remaining budget in USD. Returns a non-negative float. Returns 0.0 when the budget is fully consumed.

properties.name: cumulative_cost
properties.description: Total cost accumulated so far in USD.

properties.name: degradation_model
properties.description: Model identifier used when budget is degraded.

properties.name: degradation_threshold
properties.description: Fraction of max_cost_usd that triggers degradation (0.0 to 1.0).

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
track_usage() must accumulate costs across multiple calls for the same workflow
check_budget() must return one of: 'within_budget', 'degraded', or 'exceeded' states
get_remaining() must return a non-negative float; returns 0.0 when budget is fully consumed
When cumulative_cost reaches degradation_threshold * max_cost_usd, the enforcer must report degraded state
When cumulative_cost reaches or exceeds max_cost_usd, the enforcer must report exceeded state
Properties must be read-only — external code must not mutate budget state directly
Usage dict should support at minimum: prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_cost keys
